info.title: Marketplace Platform API
info.description: Backend API contract for the Marketplace platform.

tags.description: Authenticated checkout validation and pending-order creation

tags.name: Admin - Products
tags.description: Admin-only product reads and moderation commands

tags.name: Users (Me) - Seller Value Profile
tags.description: Manage the seller value profile of the currently authenticated user

get.description:
Returns profile fields for the current user.
Notes:
- Email/role are part of /users/me (auth identity), not changed here.

put.description:
Replaces profile fields for the current user.
Allowed fields:
- activeProfileType, simpleProfile, extendedProfile
Not allowed here:
- email, role, emailVerified, status, base user fields
Notes:
- Creates the profile if it does not exist yet (upsert).
- PATCH remains available for backward compatibility.

patch.description:
Backward-compatible alias for the profile update endpoint.
Uses the same payload and upsert behavior as PUT /api/v1/users/me/profile.

get.description: Returns all addresses for the current user. Default address is sorted first, then most recently updated.

post.description:
Creates a new address for the current user.
Enterprise rules:
- If the user has no addresses yet, the first address becomes default automatically.
- If isDefault=true is provided, current default is cleared and the new address becomes default.
- After creation we ensure that for users with at least one address, one default exists.

post.summary: Mark my order group as shipped
post.description:
Confirms shipment for the authenticated seller's order group.
The order group must currently be in PROCESSING.
The command stores trackingNumber, carrier, shippedAt, transitions the group to SHIPPED,
and recalculates the parent order status in the same transaction.

post.summary: Mark my order group as delivered
post.description:
Confirms delivery for the authenticated seller's shipped order group.
This is the current-stage seller-confirmed delivery model and is not carrier-verified or buyer-confirmed.
The command sets deliveredAt, transitions the group to DELIVERED, and recalculates the parent order status in the same transaction.

get.description:
Returns the public product list.
For authenticated users with a usable matching profile, each list item can include a personalized matchScore.
The optional sort=match_score path uses bounded application-layer ranking for the current result window.
Anonymous users and authenticated users without a usable profile still receive a normal product list, with matchScore left null.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Supported values: newest, price_asc, price_desc, match_score. match_score is only effective for authenticated users with a usable profile and otherwise falls back cleanly.

post.description:
Processes a raw Stripe webhook event through a verified and idempotent application-service path.
The provider-specific verifier authenticates the request before any payment or order mutation is attempted.
Duplicate providerEventId deliveries are ignored safely and do not reapply order payment-state changes.
A successful event finalizes reserved inventory, confirms the order exactly once, and may also create
seller settlement records exactly once for the paid order.
When payment success is applied, the platform also attempts a best-effort buyer or guest payment-success email through
the centralized email module. Refund confirmation emails are triggered internally by the refund success flow and are
not exposed as public API commands in the current stage.

Creates or safely reuses a pending payment intent for an existing buyer-owned order.
Amount and currency are always derived from the persisted order and never trusted from the client.
Only authenticated buyer-owned orders are supported for the first public launch.
Guest checkout and guest payment are intentionally disabled in this launch-hardening stage.

post.summary: Unlink my uploaded file from its current target
post.description:
Clears the current domain link of an active uploaded file.
The file remains active after unlinking and may later become an orphan-cleanup candidate under the lifecycle policy.

post.summary: Replace a linked file with another uploaded file
post.description:
Replaces a currently linked active file with another caller-owned uploaded file for the same supported target.
The current file is unlinked and left active, while the replacement file becomes the current linked file in the same transactional flow.
No replacement-history model is persisted in the current stage.

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: Current caller-owned active file identifier that is already linked to the target.

post.summary: Link my uploaded file to a supported target
post.description:
Links an active uploaded file to a supported domain target after category compatibility and target ownership validation.
The caller must own both the file and the target entity boundary they are trying to manage.
Soft-deleted files cannot be linked.

Authenticated multipart upload endpoint for reusable marketplace file assets.
The server derives validation rules from the selected category, including size limits, MIME allowlists, basic content-signature checks, and suspicious extension or MIME mismatches.
The client never chooses raw storage paths or public storage keys.
Optional related-entity metadata may be stored at upload time, but controlled attachment and replacement flows are handled through the dedicated link, unlink, and replace commands.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Upload category that selects allowed file types and maximum size.

parameters.name: relatedEntityType
parameters.description: Optional initial related entity type metadata. Dedicated link commands remain the preferred controlled attachment path.

post.description:
Validates the authenticated buyer cart for checkout without consuming inventory or clearing the cart.
The server revalidates item ownership, current prices, current inventory consistency, and required shipping or billing input.
Stale prices or invalid inventory are rejected instead of being auto-corrected.
Guest checkout is disabled for the first public launch.

Revalidates the authenticated buyer cart and creates a pending order that is awaiting payment confirmation.
On success, reserved cart inventory remains reserved, a persisted pending order is created, and cart items are removed.
Inventory is only finalized and the order is only confirmed after a verified payment-success webhook is processed.
Guest checkout is disabled for the first public launch.

Adds a variant to the effective cart for the caller.
- Authenticated callers write to their user-owned cart.
- Anonymous callers write to the guest cart resolved from cartSessionId, creating one when needed.
If the variant already exists in the cart, the quantity is increased instead of creating a duplicate row.
The current variant or product price is captured into priceSnapshot at add time and inventory is reserved transactionally.

parameters.name: cartSessionId
parameters.description: Optional guest cart session cookie. Ignored for authenticated callers. Anonymous callers without a cookie receive a new cartSessionId cookie when a cart is created.

get.description:
Verifies an email using a one-time token from the verification email.
This is a link-friendly GET endpoint. You can call it via browser.

post.description:
API-friendly email verification endpoint.
Use this from frontend/mobile clients after reading the token.
(The GET endpoint exists for link-click convenience.)

get.description:
Link-friendly endpoint for password reset emails.
This endpoint does not change the password; it only informs the client
to submit the new password via POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password.

post.description:
Resets the password using a one-time token (from email) and a new password.
After successful reset, all refresh tokens for the user are invalidated.

Registers a new user (BUYER/SELLER). A one-time email verification token is issued and the transactional verification email
is sent through the centralized email module with template rendering and delivery logging.
Notes:
- ADMIN cannot self-register
- SELLER requires companyName (vatId/iban optional)
- registration currently treats verification email delivery as part of the synchronous auth flow

Issues a new access token using the refresh token cookie.
Refresh token is rotated, returned as JSON, and set again as HttpOnly cookie named 'refreshToken'.
Frontend must send cookies (credentials: 'include').

Revokes the current refresh token (if present) and clears the refresh cookie.
Frontend must send cookies (credentials: 'include').

Authenticates a user and returns an access token.
Refresh token is issued as HttpOnly cookie named 'refreshToken' and is also returned in JSON.
Frontend must send cookies on refresh/logout (e.g. fetch with credentials: 'include').
If a guest cartSessionId cookie is present and a guest cart exists, that cart is merged into the authenticated user's cart after successful login only.
After a successful merge, the cartSessionId cookie is cleared.

parameters.name: cartSessionId
parameters.description: Optional guest cart session cookie. When present on successful login and a guest cart exists, the guest cart is merged into the authenticated user's cart and the cookie is cleared.

post.description:
Sends a password reset link if the email exists.
The reset email is rendered and delivered through the centralized email module with delivery logging.
Response is always the same to prevent email enumeration.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a user from SUSPENDED back to ACTIVE. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row. Admin users cannot be moderated by this endpoint.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a user from ACTIVE to SUSPENDED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row. Admin users cannot be moderated by this endpoint.
Admin-only moderation command that moves an APPROVED seller profile to SUSPENDED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and may demote the linked user back to BUYER.
Admin-only moderation command that moves an APPROVED seller profile to SUSPENDED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and may demote the linked user back to BUYER.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a seller profile from PENDING to REJECTED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and may demote the linked user back to BUYER.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a seller profile from PENDING to REJECTED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and may demote the linked user back to BUYER.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a seller profile from PENDING to APPROVED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and promotes the linked user to SELLER unless already ADMIN.
Admin-only moderation command that moves a seller profile from PENDING to APPROVED. Successful moderation writes an admin audit-log row and promotes the linked user to SELLER unless already ADMIN.
Admin-only moderation command that moves an ACTIVE product to INACTIVE and writes an admin audit-log row on success.
Admin-only moderation command that activates a product from REVIEW or INACTIVE. Activation requires verifiedCertificateCount >= 1 and writes an admin audit-log row on success.
Deletes expired tokens and old revoked tokens (retention-based).
Cancels overdue pending unpaid orders and releases their reserved inventory.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user's basic information.

delete.description:
Soft-deletes the user account.
Personal data is anonymized (GDPR-friendly). Keeps history integrity.

patch.description:
Updates firstName / lastName / phone.
email / role / emailVerified cannot be changed here.

get.description:
Returns the seller profile of the currently authenticated user.
Access:
- Only SELLER accounts may access this endpoint.

patch.description:
Partially updates seller profile fields.
Access:
- Only SELLER accounts.
Notes:
- If you pass a field as empty string it will be trimmed to null (where allowed).

delete.description:
Deletes an address owned by the current user.
If the deleted address was default, another address will be promoted to default if any exist.

patch.description:
Partially updates a single address owned by the current user.
If isDefault=true: clears existing default and sets this address as default (bulk updates).
If isDefault=false: unsets default and ensures another address becomes default if needed.
Sets a specific address as the default address for the current user.

Updates the authenticated seller's order group status using the explicit lifecycle rules.
Allowed transitions are limited to the business-safe forward path and selected cancellation states.
This endpoint does not create shipment or delivery metadata by itself.

get.description:
Returns the authenticated UUID-based product detail view.
When the authenticated user has a usable matching profile, the response includes matchScore and a detailed matchBreakdown.
When no usable profile is available, the product still loads successfully and matching fields remain null.

delete.description:
Removes one item from the effective cart for the caller and releases the full reserved quantity for that item.
Ownership is enforced by resolved cart context, so item identifiers from another guest or user cart are treated as not found.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Cart item identifier inside the caller's resolved cart.

parameters.name: cartSessionId
parameters.description: Optional guest cart session cookie. Ignored for authenticated callers.

patch.description:
Updates the quantity of one item in the effective cart for the caller.
Quantity must stay positive. Increasing quantity reserves only the delta. Decreasing quantity releases only the delta.
Ownership is enforced by resolved cart context, so item identifiers from another guest or user cart are treated as not found.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Cart item identifier inside the caller's resolved cart.

parameters.name: cartSessionId
parameters.description: Optional guest cart session cookie. Ignored for authenticated callers.

patch.description: Admin-only moderation command that verifies a pending certificate. Successful verification writes an admin audit-log row and may activate linked products that are waiting in REVIEW with at least one verified certificate.
Admin-only moderation command that rejects a pending certificate with an explicit reason. Successful rejection writes an admin audit-log row.

400.description: Missing rejection reason or invalid certificate state transition

get.description:
Returns internal settlement records for the authenticated seller only.
These records represent per-order-group gross, platform fee, seller net, refund impact, and payout eligibility state.
No external payout execution endpoint is exposed in the current stage.

Returns seller-owned order groups for the authenticated seller.
Order groups are the seller-facing fulfillment unit inside a buyer-facing parent order.
Cross-seller data is never returned.

get.summary: Get my order group by ID
get.description:
Returns one seller-owned order group with items, buyer summary, and shipment or delivery data.
If the order group belongs to another seller, the API returns not found.

Returns personalized product recommendations for the authenticated user.
Recommendations are ranked by computed match score, limited to ACTIVE products, and include a deterministic matchReason derived from the strongest contributing categories.
Users without a usable matching profile receive an empty list instead of an error.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recommendations to return. Defaults to the configured recommendation limit and is capped server-side.

get.description:
Returns the public storefront product detail for an ACTIVE product.
Anonymous users receive the public product view without personalization.
When an authenticated user calls the same endpoint with a usable matching profile, the response can also include matchScore and matchBreakdown.
Historical slugs redirect to the current slug when the product is still publicly visible.

get.summary: List public certificates for a product

get.description:
Returns a buyer-safe view of one persisted payment.
Access is restricted to the authenticated buyer who owns the underlying order payment.
Provider client secrets are never exposed by this read endpoint.

Returns the authenticated buyer's persisted parent orders.
Each order is buyer-facing and may contain multiple seller-owned order groups from the same checkout.
Guest order reads are intentionally not exposed in the current project stage.

Returns the authenticated buyer's full order detail, including seller-split order groups,
frozen product and address snapshots, shipment data, and the aggregated parent order status.
Access is restricted to the buyer who owns the order.

Returns ownership-safe metadata for the uploading user only.
The response includes lifecycle and current link information but never exposes raw storage keys or filesystem paths.

delete.description:
Authenticated soft-delete command for the uploader of a file.
The file remains tracked in metadata for lifecycle and cleanup purposes, but public content retrieval is blocked immediately.
Physical storage removal is handled later by internal cleanup logic, not by client-supplied paths or keys.

get.summary: Get active file content by public id
get.description:
Resolves an active file asset by opaque file identifier and streams stored content.
This is the stable public-by-id URL returned by upload and metadata responses.
Soft-deleted files are not served, and raw storage paths are never exposed.

Returns the effective cart for the caller.
- Authenticated callers use their single user-owned cart.
- Anonymous callers use the guest cart bound to the optional cartSessionId cookie.
- Anonymous GET requests without a cart cookie lazily create an empty guest cart and issue a new cartSessionId cookie.
No arbitrary cart identifier is accepted.

parameters.name: cartSessionId
parameters.description: Optional guest cart session cookie. Ignored for authenticated callers. When absent for an anonymous GET, the server creates a new guest cart and issues a cartSessionId cookie.

get.description: Returns a compact admin-only user list for moderation and support workflows. The response is intentionally operational: identity, role, status, verification state, and creation time.
Returns an admin-safe user detail view with role, status, verification state, basic profile fields, and a compact seller-profile summary when one exists.
Returns a compact admin-only settlement list showing seller allocation state, refund impact, and payout eligibility signals.
Returns a compact admin-only seller profile list for moderation and support workflows. The response includes seller identity, linked user identity, status, and creation time.
Returns an admin-safe seller profile detail view with linked user identity, company name, current moderation status, and lifecycle timestamps.
Returns a compact admin-only refund list for operational finance visibility. Refund execution remains internal in the current stage.
Returns a compact admin-only product list for moderation and support workflows. The response includes seller ownership, status, and creation time.
Returns an admin-safe product detail view with seller ownership, status, timestamps, and the verified certificate count that currently gates activation.
Returns a compact admin-only payout list for operational visibility into payout execution state. No payout mutation or finance correction tooling is exposed by this endpoint.
Returns a compact admin-only payment list for operational finance visibility. The response intentionally omits provider client secrets, webhook secrets, and raw provider payloads.
Returns an admin-only payment execution detail view. This surface is operationally useful but remains read-only and does not expose provider secrets.

Returns a compact admin-only operational order list. This surface is read-only and is intended for support visibility into buyer or guest orders, parent order status, payment status, totals, and creation time.
Returns an admin-safe order support view with parent-order identity, status, payment status, frozen shipping and billing snapshots, and seller-owned order-group fulfillment summaries.

Returns a small, accurate operational dashboard derived from existing platform data.
Run 1 intentionally exposes only metrics that are cleanly derivable from users, products, orders, and certificates.

ShipOrderGroupRequest.description: Shipment confirmation payload for a seller-owned order group.

trackingNumber.description: Seller-provided tracking number for the shipment.

carrier.description: Seller-provided carrier name, for example DHL or UPS.

OrderItemResponse.description: Final purchased line item stored inside an order group.

variantId.description: Purchased variant identifier. May be null in historical edge cases if the live variant is removed later.

pricePerUnit.description: Price per unit at purchase time in major currency units.

subtotal.description: Line subtotal at purchase time in major currency units.

productSnapshot.description: Frozen product and variant snapshot used for historical order display.

OrderProductSnapshotResponse.description: Frozen product and variant data captured at purchase time.

options.description: Variant options captured at purchase time.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code captured at purchase time.

OrderShipmentResponse.description: Shipment and delivery data stored on an order group.

shippedAt.description: Timestamp when the seller marked the order group as shipped.

deliveredAt.description: Timestamp when the seller marked the order group as delivered.

OrderVariantOptionResponse.description: Frozen variant option captured at purchase time.

type.description: Variant option type, for example size or color.

SellerBuyerSummaryResponse.description: Minimal buyer identity data exposed to the seller for a single order group.

userId.description: Authenticated buyer user identifier, if the order was created by a logged-in buyer.

guestEmail.description: Guest buyer email, if the order was created by a guest.

SellerOrderGroupResponse.description: Seller-facing detail view of a single order group.

items.description: Purchased items belonging to this seller-owned group.

totalAmount.description: Seller-visible total amount for this order group in major currency units.

shipment.description: Shipment and delivery data for this order group, if any.

buyer.description: Minimal buyer summary. Contains either userId or guestEmail depending on checkout type.

PaymentWebhookResponse.description: Outcome of a verified payment webhook after idempotency checks and payment-state evaluation.

duplicateIgnored.description: True when the webhook event had already been processed earlier.

paymentStatus.description: Current persisted payment status after processing or idempotent ignore.

CreatePaymentIntentRequest.description: Request to create a staged payment intent for an existing buyer-owned order.

paymentMethod.description: Optional normalized payment method hint, such as CARD or PAYPAL.

PaymentIntentResponse.description: Persisted payment intent response for the current payment foundation. The returned amount and currency always come from the persisted order, not client-provided money values.

status.description: Current persisted payment status, typically PENDING immediately after intent creation.

clientSecret.description: Provider client secret or staged client token used by the caller's payment UI flow.

FileMetadataResponse.description: Ownership-safe file metadata view without raw storage internals.

originalFilename.description: Original uploaded filename retained for display.

category.description: File category that controls allowed target compatibility.

url.description: Stable public-by-id content URL while the file is active. Null once the file has been soft-deleted.

deleted.description: True when the file is soft-deleted and no longer publicly retrievable.

relatedEntityType.description: Current linked entity type when the file is assigned to a supported domain target.

relatedEntityId.description: Current linked entity identifier when assigned.

deletedAt.description: Deletion timestamp when the file entered soft-delete state.

FileReplaceRequest.description: Request to replace a currently linked file with another active uploader-owned file for the same supported target.

newFileId.description: Replacement file identifier. The replacement file must already exist, remain active, be owned by the caller, and still be unlinked.

targetType.description: Supported target entity type. Both current and replacement files must be compatible with it.

targetId.description: Target entity identifier that the caller must be authorized to manage.

FileLinkRequest.description: Request to link an active uploader-owned file to a supported domain target.

targetType.description: Supported target entity type. Compatibility is validated against the file category.

targetId.description: Target entity identifier that the caller must also be authorized to manage.

FileUploadResponse.description: Business-safe metadata returned after a successful file upload.

fileId.description: Persisted file identifier used for later metadata, lifecycle, linking, and public content retrieval calls.

filename.description: Sanitized original filename retained for display.

mimeType.description: Detected MIME type after content validation.

url.description: Stable public-by-id content URL while the file remains active.

category.description: Logical upload category that drove validation and later link compatibility.

CheckoutAddressInput.description: Inline shipping or billing address supplied during checkout.

CheckoutStartRequest.description: Request to validate the resolved cart before checkout completion.

shippingAddressId.description: Saved shipping address identifier for authenticated callers. Guests must provide inline shippingAddress instead.

shippingAddress.description: Inline shipping address. Required for guests and for authenticated callers that do not use shippingAddressId.

billingSameAsShipping.description: When true or omitted, billingAddress is copied from shippingAddress.

billingAddress.description: Inline billing address when billing differs from shipping.

CheckoutAddressResponse.description: Normalized address used for checkout validation and completion responses.

CheckoutItemResponse.description: Validated cart line included in checkout start or completion responses.

currency.description: ISO currency code for the line.

CheckoutStartResponse.description: Validated checkout snapshot returned by checkout start.

readyToProceed.description: True when the cart passed current checkout validation and can proceed to completion.

ownershipType.description: Resolved cart ownership type. Current values are AUTHENTICATED or GUEST.

totalQuantity.description: Total quantity across all validated items.

currency.description: ISO currency code for the validated cart.

shippingAddress.description: Resolved shipping address used for validation.

billingAddress.description: Resolved billing address used for validation.

CheckoutCompleteRequest.description: Request to complete the resolved checkout after final validation.

shippingAddressId.description: Saved shipping address identifier for authenticated callers. Guests must provide inline shippingAddress instead.

shippingAddress.description: Inline shipping address. Required for guests and for authenticated callers that do not use shippingAddressId.

billingSameAsShipping.description: When true or omitted, billingAddress is copied from shippingAddress.

billingAddress.description: Inline billing address when billing differs from shipping.

guestEmail.description: Guest email used for guest order creation. Required for anonymous checkout completion.

paymentMethod.description: Project-stage payment method indicator. This validates completion intent but does not integrate an external payment provider yet.

CheckoutCompleteResponse.description: Completion result for the current staged checkout flow.

completionId.description: Server-generated identifier for this completion result.

status.description: Current completion status. Launch-hardening returns PENDING_PAYMENT until verified payment success confirms the order.

paymentStatus.description: Current persisted order payment status after checkout completion.

paymentMethod.description: Normalized payment method supplied by the caller.

checkout.description: Validated checkout snapshot that was completed.

AddCartItemRequest.description: Request to add a variant to the resolved cart.

variantId.description: Variant identifier to add to the cart.

quantity.description: Quantity to add. Must be at least 1.

CartItemResponse.description: One line item in the resolved cart.

variantId.description: Variant identifier represented by this cart line.

quantity.description: Current reserved quantity for this cart line.

priceSnapshot.description: Unit price captured when the item was added to cart.

currency.description: ISO currency code for the line item.

lineTotal.description: Computed line total based on priceSnapshot multiplied by quantity.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp of the cart line.

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp of the cart line.

VoidEnvelope.description: Envelope for endpoints that do not return a payload (data is null).

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error envelope returned by the API (status='error').

fields.email: must be a well-formed email address

AuthTokensResponse.description: Envelope for auth token responses. Refresh token is sent as HttpOnly cookie and also returned in JSON.

message.description: Optional human-readable message. Can be null.

refreshToken.description: Refresh token. Also issued as HttpOnly cookie named 'refreshToken'.

user.description: Authenticated user. Present on login, can be null on refresh.

status.description: Resulting user status after the admin command.

reason.description: Human-readable suspension reason recorded in the admin audit log.

status.description: Resulting seller profile status after the admin command.

reason.description: Human-readable rejection reason recorded in the admin audit log.

status.description: Resulting product status after the admin command.

UpdateSellerOrderGroupStatusRequest.description: Explicit seller lifecycle transition request for an order group.

status.description: Target order-group status. Shipment and delivery metadata should use the dedicated ship and deliver commands.

UpdateCartItemRequest.description: Request to replace the quantity of an existing cart item.

quantity.description: New quantity for the cart item. Must stay positive; zero does not delete the item.

status.description: Resulting certificate status after the moderation command.

verifiedByAdminId.description: Admin user id that verified the certificate, if verified.

verifiedAt.description: Verification timestamp, if the certificate is verified.

rejectionReason.description: Stored rejection reason when the certificate is rejected.

reason.description: Human-readable rejection reason recorded in the admin audit log.

SellerSettlementResponse.description: Seller-facing internal settlement view for one seller-owned order group.

orderGroupId.description: Seller-owned order-group identifier the settlement belongs to.

grossAmount.description: Gross marketplace amount allocated to this order group before fee deduction.

platformFeeAmount.description: Persisted platform fee amount for this settlement.

netAmount.description: Persisted seller net amount intended for payout once safely payable.

refundedAmount.description: Refunded amount already applied against this settlement.

status.description: Current settlement status such as PENDING, ELIGIBLE, PAID, FAILED, or CANCELLED.

adjustmentRequired.description: True when refund-related adjustment exists and payout must not proceed blindly.

eligibleAt.description: Timestamp when the settlement first became payout-eligible, when available.

primaryImage.description: Primary product image URL when available.

matchReason.description: Deterministic explanation derived from the highest contributing matching categories.

ProductListItemDto.description: Public product list item with optional personalized matching.

matchScore.description: Personalized match score from 0 to 100. Null when the request is anonymous or no usable matching profile is available.

CategoryMatchDto.description: Category-level contribution within a match breakdown.

userPreference.description: User preference value for this category on a 0 to 100 scale.

productSupported.description: Whether the product supports this category through verified linked certificates.

contribution.description: Contribution of this category to the overall score on a 0 to 100 scale.

MatchBreakdownDto.description: Detailed category-level explanation behind a personalized match score.

categories.description: Per-category matching details used to explain the score.

matchedCategories.description: Matching categories that contributed positively for the product.

ProductDetailDto.description: Authenticated product detail view with optional personalized matching.

matchScore.description: Personalized match score from 0 to 100. Null when no usable matching profile is available.

matchBreakdown.description: Detailed personalized matching breakdown. Present only when a usable matching profile is available.

PaymentStatusResponse.description: Buyer-safe persisted payment view with payment execution state only. Settlement, refund, and payout internals are intentionally excluded.

status.description: Current persisted payment status such as PENDING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, PARTIALLY_REFUNDED, or REFUNDED.

OrderSummaryResponse.description: Buyer-facing summary of a persisted order.

status.description: Aggregated parent order status derived from child order groups.

paymentStatus.description: Current payment status retained on the order.

total.description: Buyer-visible order total in major currency units.

OrderAddressResponse.description: Frozen order address snapshot retained on the order.

OrderDetailResponse.description: Buyer-facing order detail with seller-split order groups and frozen purchase snapshots.

userId.description: Buyer user identifier for authenticated orders. Null for guest orders.

guestEmail.description: Guest email retained on guest-created orders. Null for authenticated buyer orders.

status.description: Aggregated parent order status derived from child order groups.

paymentStatus.description: Payment status stored on the order.

subtotal.description: Order subtotal in major currency units.

shippingCost.description: Order shipping cost in major currency units.

tax.description: Order tax amount in major currency units.

total.description: Order total in major currency units.

shippingAddress.description: Frozen shipping address snapshot stored at purchase time.

billingAddress.description: Frozen billing address snapshot stored at purchase time, if present.

groups.description: Seller-split order groups that make up the parent order.

OrderGroupResponse.description: Buyer-visible seller-specific fulfillment group inside a parent order.

sellerId.description: Seller user identifier responsible for this group.

status.description: Current fulfillment status for this seller-owned order group.

subtotal.description: Order-group subtotal in major currency units.

shippingCost.description: Order-group shipping cost in major currency units.

shipment.description: Shipment and delivery data for this seller-owned fulfillment group, if any.

items.description: Purchased items belonging to this seller-owned group.

CartResponse.description: Resolved cart for the current caller.

userId.description: Authenticated owner identifier when this is a user cart.

sessionId.description: Guest cart session identifier when this is a guest cart. Null for authenticated carts.

totalQuantity.description: Sum of all cart item quantities.

items.description: Cart line items ordered by creation time.

role.description: Current platform role, for example BUYER, SELLER, or ADMIN.

emailVerified.description: Whether the account email has already been verified.
Whether the account email has been verified.

sellerProfile.description: Compact seller profile summary when the user also owns a seller profile.

verifiedCertificateCount.description: Count of verified certificates currently linked to the product.

FileDeleteResponse.description: Soft-delete result for one uploader-owned file asset.

deleted.description: True when the file is now soft-deleted and public content retrieval is blocked.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp when the file entered soft-delete state. Physical storage cleanup may happen later.
